zHDNS rdatasets (an rdataset is a set of rdatas of a given type and class)
DifferingCoversz~An attempt was made to add a DNS SIG/RRSIG whose covered type
    is not the same as that of the other rdatas in the rdataset.N

Rdatasetz
A DNS rdataset.)
rdclass
rdtype
covers
ttlr
Create a new rdataset of the specified class and type.
        *rdclass*, an ``int``, the rdataclass.
        *rdtype*, an ``int``, the rdatatype.
        *covers*, an ``int``, the covered rdatatype.
        *ttl*, an ``int``, the TTL.
        N)
super
__init__r
selfr
	__class__r
Rdataset.__init__c
_cloner
objr
Rdataset._clonec
Perform TTL minimization.
        Set the TTL of the rdataset to be the lesser of the set's current
        TTL or the specified TTL.  If the set contains no rdatas, set the TTL
        to the specified TTL.
        *ttl*, an ``int`` or ``str``.
        r
dnsr
make
update_ttlL
Rdataset.update_ttlNc
Add the specified rdata to the rdataset.
        If the optional *ttl* parameter is supplied, then
        ``self.update_ttl(ttl)`` will be called prior to adding the rdata.
        *rd*, a ``dns.rdata.Rdata``, the rdata
        *ttl*, an ``int``, the TTL.
        Raises ``dns.rdataset.IncompatibleTypes`` if the type and class
        do not match the type and class of the rdataset.
        Raises ``dns.rdataset.DifferingCovers`` if the type is a signature
        type and the covered type does not match that of the rdataset.
        Nr
	rdatatypeZ
RRSIGZ
SIGr
NONEr

Rdataset.intersection_updatec
Add all rdatas in other to self.
        *other*, a ``dns.rdataset.Rdataset``, the rdataset from which
        to update.
        N)

<DNS 
 rdataset: 
to_text
rdataclassr
ctextr
__repr__
Rdataset.__repr__c
__str__
Rdataset.__str__c
isinstancer
__eq__r#
Rdataset.__eq__c
__ne__
Rdataset.__ne__TFc
r||	
Convert the rdataset into DNS zone file format.
        See ``dns.name.Name.choose_relativity`` for more information
        on how *origin* and *relativize* determine the way names
        are emitted.
        Any additional keyword arguments are passed on to the rdata
        ``to_text()`` method.
        *name*, a ``dns.name.Name``.  If name is not ``None``, emit RRs with
        *name* as the owner name.
        *origin*, a ``dns.name.Name`` or ``None``, the origin for relative
        names.
        *relativize*, a ``bool``.  If ``True``, names will be relativized
        to *origin*.
        *override_rdclass*, a ``dns.rdataclass.RdataClass`` or ``None``.
        If not ``None``, use this class instead of the Rdataset's class.
        *want_comments*, a ``bool``.  If ``True``, emit comments for rdata
        which have them.  The default is ``False``.
        Nr4
{}{}{} {}
%s%s%d %s %s %s%s
origin
relativize
choose_relativityr*
StringIOr
write
formatr
Z	rdcommentr
getvalue)
namer=
override_rdclassZ
want_comments
ntextZ
padr(
extrar
Rdataset.to_textc
}	|	D
)	at
Convert the rdataset to wire format.
        *name*, a ``dns.name.Name`` is the owner name to use.
        *file* is the file where the name is emitted (typically a
        BytesIO file).
        *compress*, a ``dict``, is the compression table to use.  If
        ``None`` (the default), names will not be compressed.
        *origin* is a ``dns.name.Name`` or ``None``.  If the name is
        relative and origin is not ``None``, then *origin* will be appended
        to it.
        *override_rdclass*, an ``int``, is used as the class instead of the
        class of the rdataset.  This is useful when rendering rdatasets
        associated with dynamic updates.
        *want_shuffle*, a ``bool``.  If ``True``, then the order of the
        Rdatas within the Rdataset will be shuffled before rendering.
        Returns an ``int``, the number of records emitted.
        NFr
!HHIH
seekr@
SEEK_ENDr
to_wire
struct
packr
list
randomZ
shuffler
tell)
file
compressr=
want_shuffler
stuff
start
endr
Rdataset.to_wirec
zaReturns ``True`` if this rdataset matches the specified class,
        type, and covers.
        TF)
match.
Rdataset.matchc
Return rdatas in a valid processing order according to the type's
        specification.  For example, MX records are in preference order from
        lowest to highest preferences, with items of the same preference
        shuffled.
        For types that do not define a processing order, the rdatas are
        simply shuffled.
        r
_processing_order
iterr/
processing_order8
Rdataset.processing_order)
NNTNF)
NNNT)

ImmutableRdatasetz
An immutable DNS rdataset.c
z9Create an immutable rdataset from the specified rdataset.N)

ImmutableRdataset.unionc

ImmutableRdataset.difference)
_clone_classr
]*}	t
Create an rdataset with the specified class, type, and TTL, and with
    the specified list of rdatas in text format.
    *idna_codec*, a ``dns.name.IDNACodec``, specifies the IDNA
    encoder/decoder to use; if ``None``, the default IDNA 2003
    encoder/decoder is used.
    *origin*, a ``dns.name.Name`` (or ``None``), the
    origin to use for relative names.
    *relativize*, a ``bool``.  If true, name will be relativized.
    *relativize_to*, a ``dns.name.Name`` (or ``None``), the origin to use
    when relativizing names.  If not set, the *origin* value will be used.
    Returns a ``dns.rdataset.Rdataset`` object.
    )
RdataClassr
Z	RdataTyper
rdata
	from_textr
text_rdatasZ
idna_codecr=
relativize_to
from_text_list
Create an rdataset with the specified class, type, and TTL, and with
    the specified rdatas in text format.
    Returns a ``dns.rdataset.Rdataset`` object.
    )
Create an rdataset with the specified TTL, and with
    the specified list of rdata objects.
    Returns a ``dns.rdataset.Rdataset`` object.
    r
rdata list must not be emptyN)
ValueErrorr
rdatasrr

Create an rdataset with the specified TTL, and with
    the specified rdata objects.
    Returns a ``dns.rdataset.Rdataset`` object.
    )
